concepts.title: ZPLS Call Bridge (Shared Database Node)
concepts.summary: The Call Bridge is a standalone service that enables call routing between different ZPLS modules or node groups during internet outages. Without it, users on different ZPLS nodes cannot call each other in survivability mode. Designers should highlight this as critical infrastructure — if the Call Bridge goes down, inter-node calling fails entirely.
key_points: Enables inter-node and inter-node-group calling during outages
Runs on a standalone Node server accessible to all ZPLS modules
Not required for calls within the same node group
Synchronizes state information across ZPLS modules

user_roles.title: Account Owner or Admin with Edit Privileges
user_roles.summary: Deploying and assigning the Call Bridge requires admin access to both Node Management (for deployment) and Phone System Management (for assignment). These are separate admin portal sections, meaning the setup spans two different navigation paths.
key_points: Node Management > Modules for deployment
Phone System Management > Company Info for assignment
Must have account settings edit privilege

task_flows.title: Deploy Call Bridge Service
task_flows.summary: Admin navigates: Node Management > Modules > Phone Local Survivability > Services tab > Add Services > Call Bridge. Configuration: select Zoom Node Phone server, enter Internal IP, optionally add Internal Domain prefix. This flow is distinct from standard ZPLS module deployment.
key_points: Path: Node Management > Modules > Phone Local Survivability > Services > Add Services
Must select target Node server from dropdown
Internal IP must match the Node server's configured address

task_flows.title: Assign Call Bridge to ZPLS Node or Node Group
task_flows.summary: Admin navigates: Phone System Management > Company Info > Account Settings > Zoom Node > Local Survivability > Shared Database Nodes > Edit. Select ZPLS Node or Node Group from tabbed interface, then manually sync data to complete assignment.
key_points: Path: Phone System Management > Company Info > Account Settings > Zoom Node > Shared Database Nodes > Edit
Tabbed selection: Node or Node Group
Must manually sync after assignment

constraints.title: Call Bridge Network Resilience Requirement
constraints.summary: If connectivity to the Call Bridge is lost during survivability mode, originating nodes cannot locate destination nodes, causing call failure. This means the Call Bridge is a single point of failure for inter-node calling — designers should ensure this risk is communicated in the admin UI.
key_points: Call Bridge is a single point of failure for inter-node calls
Network topology resilience directly impacts calling availability
Within same node group, calls work without Call Bridge

constraints.title: Call Bridge Server Requirements
constraints.summary: Call Bridge must be deployed on a standalone Node server accessible to all ZPLS modules. It cannot share a server with other ZPLS services. This is a hardware/resource constraint with UX implications for capacity planning.
key_points: Must be on a standalone server
Must be accessible to all ZPLS modules
Cannot co-reside with other ZPLS services on same server

ux_patterns.title: Two-Phase Service Deployment Pattern
ux_patterns.summary: Call Bridge deployment follows a two-phase pattern: (1) deploy the service on a Node server, then (2) assign it to ZPLS nodes/groups. Each phase uses a different admin portal section. This separation of deploy-vs-assign is consistent with other ZPLS services (SBC, route groups) and could be designed as a unified 'Link Service' pattern.
key_points: Phase 1: Deploy service (Node Management)
Phase 2: Assign to target (Phone System Management)
Consistent pattern across ZPLS services
